Chemical_2_Common_Products: Window cleaners; glass cleaners; some bathroom cleaners
Mechanism_of_Harm: Chlorine hypochlorite oxidizes ammonia producing chloramine gas which attacks respiratory system and lungs
Health_Effects: Eye irritation; throat irritation; coughing; shortness of breath; chest pain; wheezing; nausea; pneumonia; pulmonary edema; death in severe cases

Primary_Reaction_Products: Chlorine gas; oxygen gas; water; sodium chloride
Mechanism_of_Harm: Bleach oxidizes hydrogen peroxide violently producing oxygen gas with intense heat and potential chlorine gas formation
Health_Effects: Respiratory tract irritation; skin irritation; eye irritation; chemical burns; potential blast injuries

Primary_Reaction_Products: Chlorine gas; sodium acetate; hypochlorous acid; water
Mechanism_of_Harm: Acetic acid lowers pH which converts hypochlorous acid to chlorine gas via acid dissociation
Health_Effects: Eye irritation; throat irritation; respiratory tract irritation; pulmonary edema; coughing; wheezing; potential fatality at high exposure

Chemical_2_Common_Products: Toilet bowl cleaners; muriatic acid; some pool cleaners
Mechanism_of_Harm: Acid reacts with hypochlorite ion producing chlorine gas which is absorbed through lungs and skin
Health_Effects: Respiratory tract damage; reactive airways dysfunction syndrome (RADS); lung edema; pulmonary fibrosis; airway constriction; potential fatality

Mechanism_of_Harm: Bleach oxidizes isopropanol to form chloroform and related compounds through oxidative cleavage
Health_Effects: Anesthesia; unconsciousness; cardiac arrhythmia ('sudden sniffer death'); dizziness; headache; liver damage; kidney damage; respiratory irritation; potential cancer risk

Mechanism_of_Harm: Similar oxidative mechanism to isopropanol producing chloroform and related halogenated compounds
Health_Effects: Anesthesia; unconsciousness; cardiac arrhythmia; dizziness; liver damage; kidney damage; nervous system damage; potential cancer

Mechanism_of_Harm: Phosphoric acid reduces pH causing conversion of hypochlorite to chlorine gas
Health_Effects: Eye irritation; respiratory tract irritation; dizziness; nausea; potential fatality at high exposure

Chemical_1_Common_Products: Disinfectants; wound cleaners; some whitening products
Primary_Reaction_Products: Peracetic acid (peroxyacetic acid); water; acetic acid
Mechanism_of_Harm: Formation of peracetic acid as an equilibrium product through acid-catalyzed reaction; slow at room temperature without catalyst
Health_Effects: Eye irritation; skin irritation; respiratory tract irritation; severe irritant to mucous membranes; corrosive to tissues

Mechanism_of_Harm: Formation of primary explosive acetone peroxide through nucleophilic addition especially with acid catalyst; highly sensitive to friction and shock
Health_Effects: Explosive detonation; blast injuries; thermal burns; formation of highly unstable white crystalline solid

Chemical_1_Common_Products: Pool shock; pool chemicals; some disinfectants
Mechanism_of_Harm: Incompatible chlorinating agents react exothermically causing rapid chlorine gas release and ignition
Health_Effects: Fire; explosion; thermal burns; respiratory tract injury; potential fatality

Chemical_2_Common_Products: Pool pH decreasers; some pool cleaners
Mechanism_of_Harm: Acid releases chlorine gas from hypochlorite ion through protonation
Health_Effects: Respiratory tract damage; reactive airways dysfunction; pulmonary edema; airway constriction; potential fatality

Chemical_1_Common_Products: Drain cleaners; oven cleaners; caustic soda
Mechanism_of_Harm: Dissolution of solid NaOH in water is highly exothermic releasing substantial heat
Health_Effects: Severe chemical burns; potential boiling of water causing splash injuries; eye damage; skin damage; respiratory irritation

Chemical_2_Common_Products: Aluminum foil; some metals in pipes
Mechanism_of_Harm: Aluminum reacts with sodium hydroxide in aqueous solution producing hydrogen gas and heat; hydrogen is flammable and explosive
Health_Effects: Thermal burns; blast injuries from hydrogen explosion; chemical burns; boiling water splash

Primary_Reaction_Products: Chloramine gas (if bleach also present); hydroxide compounds
Mechanism_of_Harm: If bleach is also in the drain or on surfaces sodium hydroxide + ammonia can produce chloramine gas
Health_Effects: Respiratory tract irritation; eye irritation; toxic gas exposure

Chemical_1_Common_Products: Some drain cleaners (Liquid Lightning); battery acid
Mechanism_of_Harm: Dissolution of sulfuric acid in water is extremely exothermic causing rapid temperature increase and potential spattering
Health_Effects: Severe chemical burns; eye damage; potential blindness; respiratory tract irritation; tooth enamel erosion

Mechanism_of_Harm: Neutralization reaction is extremely exothermic and violent
Health_Effects: Severe thermal burns; explosion risk; spattering hazard; respiratory irritation

Mechanism_of_Harm: Exothermic decomposition of bleach accelerated by alkaline conditions
Health_Effects: Thermal burns; eye irritation; respiratory irritation

Mechanism_of_Harm: Dissolution of solid potassium hydroxide in water is highly exothermic
Health_Effects: Severe chemical burns; eye damage; skin damage; potential blindness if contacted

Mechanism_of_Harm: Bleach oxidizes MEK producing chloroform and related compounds
Health_Effects: Respiratory irritation; headache; dizziness; liver damage; kidney damage; potential carcinogen
